Hi Simran,

*NephroPlus delivered strong revenue growth of 32% YoY in FY26, driven by
13/74% YoY growth in India/International markets, with Ebitda margins
expanding ~155bps YoY to 23.7% in FY26 due to higher International markets
revenue contribution (42% in FY26 vs 32% in FY25), leading to 41% YoY
Ebitda growth. Mgmt reiterated its organic revenue growth guidance of
15-20% over the next 3-4 years, led by improvement in capacity utilisation,
capacity addition, and annual price hikes in PPP contracts, with Ebitda
margin expansion of 100bps p.a. driven by higher International revenue mix
and price hikes. We forecast Revenue/Ebitda/EPS Cagr of 19/24/39% over
FY26-29ii and largely maintain our FY27/28ii Ebitda estimates. The stock is
currently trading at 21/16x FY27/28ii pre-Ind AS Ebitda and our TP of Rs745
(22% upside) is based on 19x 2YF EV/Ebitda (pre-Ind AS). Maintain BUY.*

*No. of sessions grew at 16.6% YoY in FY26,* driven by center additions of
22/10/2 in India/Philippines/Uzbekistan during the year as well as capacity
utilisation improvement from 72% to 75%. With center additions and
improvement in capacity utilisation, NephroPlus served 36,981
patients/guests (up 11.8% YoY) in FY26 while the frequency per week also
improved from 2.23/week in FY25 to 2.65/week in FY26. Mgmt indicated that
it plans to add 50/15 centers p.a. in India/Philippines excluding any large
PPP contract and M&A. NephroPlus has now become the 2nd largest player in
Philippines & there are enough opportunities to further consolidate the
market given 500-600 centers are still run by unorganised players. We
expect NephroPlus’ no. of sessions to grow at 14% Cagr over FY26-29ii.

*Realisation per session grew at 13.4% YoY in FY26,* driven by higher
international revenue contribution (42% in FY26 vs 32% in FY25), full year
impact of the 44% reimbursement price hike in Philippines (in PHP terms
w.e.f. Oct’24), 33% price hike in CGHS implemented from 3QFY26, and 4-5%
annual price hike in PPP contracts. We expect NephroPlus’ realisation per
session to grow at 5% Cagr over FY26-29ii, largely driven by increase in
international revenue mix and annual price hikes of 4-5% in PPP biz.

*India cost leadership playbook applied to higher priced geographies is
driving structural margin expansion:* As international revenue contribution
increases from 42% in FY26 to 49% by FY29ii, we expect pre-Ind AS Ebitda
margins to expand from 23% in FY26ii to 26% in FY29ii, with RoIC (post-tax)
improving from ~18% in FY26 to ~30% in FY29ii.

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Simran Thakkar <simran@beascapital.in>
Date: Thu, May 21, 2026 at 8:12 PM
Subject: Fw: Apollo Hospitals (4QFY26): Strong quarter with Rev/Ebitda
beating estimates by 2/5% resp.
To: Chiraag Kapil <ceekay@muns.io>

Get Outlook for Android <https://aka.ms/AAb9ysg>
------------------------------
*From:* Rahul Jeewani, IIFLCAP <rahul.jeewani@iiflcap.com>
*Sent:* Wednesday, May 20, 2026 10:49:11 PM
*To:* Simran Thakkar <simran@beascapital.in>
*Subject:* Apollo Hospitals (4QFY26): Strong quarter with Rev/Ebitda
beating estimates by 2/5% resp.

Hi Simran,

# *Apollo’s 4Q overall revenue grew 18% YoY, 2% ahead of IIFLe*, driven by
16/20/24% YoY growth in the Hospitals business, Pharmacy segment (Apollo
HealthCo), and AHLL resp. Overall / ex-24/7 Ebitda grew 31% / 17% YoY in
4Q, 5% ahead of IIFLe, with the Ebitda beat primarily driven by the topline
beat from the Hospitals business and higher-than-expected margins for AHLL.

# *Hospital business growth of 16% YoY in 4Q was strong (vs IIFLe of 13%
growth)* and was driven by IP volume/price/case mix growth of ~7/4/5% resp.
ARPP/ARPOB grew 9/12% YoY in 4Q. Apollo commissioned 4 new hospitals in
4QFY26 with 185 operational beds across Pune, Kolkata, Hyderabad and Delhi
out of the 855 census beds across these 4 new hospitals (remaining 670 beds
will be commissioned over the next few quarters).

# Despite commissioning of additional capacity, Apollo’s Hospital occupancy
was steady at ~68% in 4Q. Growth in the Hospitals business was resilient
across markets with Tamil-Nadu, AP/Telangana, Karnataka, Eastern & Western
cluster revenues growing 14-21% YoY in 4Q, with only Northern cluster being
slightly tepid at 11% growth in 4Q.

# Hospital services’ Ebitda margins declined ~43/92bps YoY/QoQ to 23.9% in
4Q and was largely in-line with IIFLe of 24%. While reported Hospital
Ebitda grew 14% YoY in 4Q, we note that the 4 newly commissioned hospitals
had a combined Ebitda loss of Rs414m in 4Q which dragged reported Hospital
Ebitda margins by ~160bps in 4Q. *Excluding new hospital losses, Hospital
services’ Ebitda margins were 25.5% in 4Q and improved ~120bps YoY*.
40/45/9/6% of overall IP revenue was contributed by
Cash/Insurance/PSU-Govt/International patients resp. in FY26.

# Update on Hospital expansion plans: Apollo will add 1,000 beds (835
census beds) in FY27 and 2,415 beds (1,970 census beds) over FY29-30. Total
planned capex is Rs83bn for 2,800 census beds, with balance Rs51bn yet to
be spent.

# *Apollo HealthCo/Pharmacy business (Offline + Online + Digital Health)
revenue grew 20% YoY in 4Q* and came in-line with our estimate of 20%
growth. Offline Pharmacy business’ Ebitda margins remained steady QoQ at
7.7% in 4Q, thereby leading to 20% YoY Ebitda growth for the Offline
Pharmacy business in 4Q.

# HealthCo Platform GMV (restated) grew 20% YoY to ~Rs5.28bn in 4Q.
Platform (Online Pharmacy & 24/7) Revenue to GMV ratio increased QoQ from
60% to 62.5% in 4Q. *Online Pharmacy & 24/7 Ebitda losses decreased 69/42%
YoY/QoQ to Rs391m in 4Q* (vs Rs1,252/675m in 4QFY25/3QFY26 resp) and came
in ~5% below our expectations of Rs412m.

# HealthCo (incl. Keimed) reported revenue of Rs190bn in FY26, with Ebitda
margins of 4.8% (ex-24/7 margins of 6.5%). Mgmt expects the combined entity
(HealthCo + Keimed) to achieve Rs250bn annualised revenue run-rate in
4QFY27 with 6.5-7% overall Ebitda margins (vs 4.8% in FY26).

# AHLL revenue grew 24% YoY in 4Q (8% above IIFLe), driven by 52/13/11%
growth in the Diagnostics/Primary Care/Specialty Care resp. AHLL’s Ebitda
margins also improved 328/506bps YoY/QoQ to 15.3% (above IIFLe of 10.5%)
driven by strong growth in the Diagnostics business and margin expansion
for the Specialty Care segment.

# Apollo’s consol. Ebitda margins improved ~150bps YoY to 15.3% (~46bps
above IIFLe) in 4Q, led by ~330bps YoY improvement in AHLL margins, which
was further aided by 69% YoY decline in Online Pharmacy & 24/7 Ebitda
losses. Apollo’s overall Ebitda grew 31% YoY in 4Q and came-in 5% above
IIFLe. Ebitda (ex-24/7 losses) grew 17% YoY in 4Q, 5% above IIFLe.

# Adj. PAT grew 36% YoY in 4Q, led by Ebitda growth of 31%, and was 5%
above IIFLe. Apollo’s net debt stood at Rs8.6bn as of Mar’26 with ND/Ebitda
of ~0.23x, compared with a net cash position of Rs3bn as of Dec’25. The
increase in debt was primarily driven by borrowings undertaken for the
Hospital services division.

# We have a BUY rating on Apollo and will revert post the earnings call
scheduled for tomorrow at 3pm IST. Diamond Pass Registration
<https://services.choruscall.in/DiamondPassRegistration/register?confirmationNumber=2883573&linkSecurityString=180ef1fc42>
.

*From:* Rahul Jeewani, IIFLCAP <rahul.jeewani@iiflcap.com>

*To:* Simran Thakkar <simran@beascapital.in>
*Subject:* Eris Lifesciences – Banking on Insulins and GLP-1 to drive
growth – BUY

Hi Simran,

*Eris’ overall grew only 7% YoY in 4Q and came-in 6% below IIFLe, largely
on account of Swiss’ RoW business declining 8/23% YoY/QoQ (32% below IIFLe)
due to the Middle East conflict. Ebitda margin came in at 36.2% (75bps
below IIFLe) in 4Q, leading to 8% Ebitda miss vs IIFLe. Eris’ reported
India business growth of 9% in 4Q and 8% in FY26 was muted owing to
rationalisation of the TGx business, while injectables scale-down will
again impact reported India growth by ~200-250bps in FY27. Mgmt guided
India business to grow at ~1.3x CVM growth in FY27, and RoW business to
grow at 18-20%, implying mid-teens consol. revenue growth for FY27 with
consol. Ebitda margins of ~36% (2H>1H). We downgrade our FY27/28ii Ebitda
estimates by ~2% owing to delay in the EU-CDMO business. Forecast overall
Revenue/Ebitda to clock 13/14% Cagr over FY26-29ii, driven by 11/20% Cagr
in India/RoW business. Our TP of Rs1,610 (10% upside) is based on ~25x 2YF
ex-amort EPS.*

*We expect Eris' India business to clock ~11% Cagr over FY26-29ii,* driven
by robust traction in Sema (secondary sales tracking Rs40-50m/month in
Apr-May'26), MS improvement in insulins (Aspart launch in FY27) and the
Bhopal facility now being fully commissioned removing the key supply
bottleneck. Insulin MS of Eris improved from 12% in Apr’25 to 16% in
Mar’26. Eris achieved #1/2 rank by volume/value with 22/13% MS in Apr’26 in
the India Sema market. Tailwinds are expected from 2QFY27 onwards, with
in-sourcing of Sema pens (Ph-1 capacity of 5m units p.a.) expected to drive
margin expansion. Mgmt expects GLP-1 patient volumes of 1-1.5m for the IPM
by end-FY27 and the market will largely consolidate among 6-7 players.

*Mgmt guided 18-20% growth for the RoW business (ex-CDMO):* FY26 was a
muted year for Swiss’ RoW business with 7% YoY revenue growth and margins
declining ~200bps YoY to ~31.6% on account of EU-CDMO investments. The
EU-GMP non-compliance observations (procedural in nature) received in
Apr’26 pushed the Rs1.2-1.4bn CDMO revenue from FY27ii, while the existing
business remains unaffected. We bake in ~20% revenue Cagr for Swiss’ RoW
business over FY26-29ii.

*Eris’ OCF/Ebitda at 48% in FY26 was depressed, vs an avg. of 80% over
FY21-25,* owing to 8-9 months of strategic RM inventory stocking and higher
receivables, which should normalise over the next 2 quarters. With a better
Ebitda-to-OCF conversion of ~75% over FY27-29ii, we expect Eris’ net debt
to decline from Rs22.4bn in FY26 to Rs18.5/12.4bn in FY27/28ii.

*From:* Aditya Bhartia <aditya.bhartia@investec.com>

*Subject:* First Take: Whirlpool of India - Tough end to a challenging year

[image: Image]

Whirlpool of India (WHIR.NS)

Price: INR855 | Target: INR1,315 | Rec: Hold

Tough end to a challenging year

Whirlpool’s Q4 FY26 EBITDA (Rs1.2bn, -34% YoY) missed our/ consensus
estimates by c.30%. While Elica fared well, standalone business
disappointed. The miss was on account of a) a sharp contraction in gross
margins (-3.8ppt YoY, 30.8% vs our est: 33.8%) and b) higher opex (+14%
YoY). Thus, EBITDA margins fell 3.6ppt YoY to 5.6% (vs our and consensus
est of 8.3-8.6%). In Q3 FY26 conf call, mgmt had cautioned about
possibility of delay in passing on higher costs due to new BEE norms in
Refs and ACs (applicable from 1st Jan 2026). Revs, though, were slightly
ahead of est (+9% YoY vs est: +6% YoY). For FY26, Whirlpool’s performance
was disappointing, with revs remaining flattish and EBITDAM contracting
1ppt YoY. Thus, PAT declined 18% YoY to c.Rs3bn, similar to FY17 levels.
Management commentary on a) competition, b) demand for summer centric
products, and c) RM Inflation and its pass through to consumer will be the
key

Weak standalone (ex-Elica) results
 Whirlpool’s standalone revs grew 7% YoY in Q4, in-line with our est.
 However, steep contraction in standalone gross margins (-4ppt YoY) and
higher opex (+13% YoY) led to c.4ppt YoY decline in standalone EBITDA
margins (to 4.9% vs our est: 8.2%).

Elica fares well
 Elica’s revenues grew 31% YoY in Q4, partly aided by a weak base (-6% YoY
revenue decline in Q4 FY25).
 Elica’s gross margins and EBITDA margins expanded 1.5ppt and 2ppt YoY
respectively in Q4.

*Subject:* PI Industries: Operating miss, recovery some time away - Hold

[image: Image]

PI Industries (PIIL.NS)

Price: INR3,125 | Target: INR3,000 | Rec: Hold

Another weak quarter for PI, with EBITDA miss of ~10% vs street. est.
Operating performance was weighed down by CSM Ag (74% rev share, rev
decline of 15%YoY) and domestic Ag (20% rev share, 9%YoY decline). CSM
business has been on downward spiral for five consecutive quarters now.
Recall, Kumiai (top customer) has guided for an -11%YoY decline in AXEEV
sales (key molecule for PI) in FY26E (Nov’25-Oct’26), driven by expected
entry of generics in the US (~70% of volumes) and pricing pressure. Mgmt.
commentary of a return to revenue growth in FY27E (vs -16%YoY in FY26) does
little to reassure us about the clarity and credibility of the recovery
path. Growth is expected to be more led by 2H, based on customer schedules
per management. Moreover, mgmt. commentary implied a decline in revenue
from new products (~18% share) in FY26E. On the contrary, Pharma business
(~5% rev share in FY26) seems to be out of gestation period with revenue
growth at 40%YoY in FY26. Mgmt. is also expanding to electronics chemicals
with 2-3 years to get to a decent scale and potential of $800m revenue
run-rate in next 5-6 years.  While we like long term prospects of PI, with
demand trends weakening for Pyroxa and the ex Pyroxa business taking longer
to gain meaningful traction, PI’s will transition thorough a lower growth
phase (~12% EPS CAGR over FY26-28E). Against this backdrop, valuation at
34x/29x FY27/28E EPS (5 years avg. 35x) seems punchy to us. Retain Hold.

*Subject:* Samvardhana Motherson International Ltd: Strong quarter,
building blocks falling in place - Buy

[image: Image]

Samvardhana Motherson International Ltd (SAMD.NS)

Price: INR131 | Target: INR160 | Rec: Buy

Q4 EBITDA of Rs38bn (+42% YoY) was ~15% ahead of our estimates led by
strong performance across most businesses. Consol. revenue grew by 17%YoY
to Rs343bn, 5% ahead of our est. EBITDAM of 11.1% (+200bps YoY) was ahead
of our estimate of 10%. Cost optimisation initiatives in the polymer
business (EU), strong performance of wiring harness and op leverage largely
drove margin expansion. Mgmt. commentary was upbeat on demand, notably on
CVs in developed markets, supported by multiple program launches with EU
OEMs, a strong order book of US$ 96bn (7x FY26 revenue) across businesses,
and improving traction in the Emerging Business segment.. SAMIL’s Emerging
Business (now ~15% of revenue) delivered strong traction in FY26, growing
50% YoY, driven by around 40% growth in aerospace and a 7.5x jump in the
consumer electronics segment off a low base. Our SAMIL upgrade (note
<https://securities.investec.com/research/api/document/CRUS2_469887/W/pdf?access_token=e24de6fb5757a1339f06faf52f60e3ef>)
was premised on a) improving global OEM outlook after a weak CY25, b)
ramp-up of aerospace and consumer electronics business, c) multiple
rerating given increase in emerging business proportion and India revenue
mix. Valuation of ~20x FY28E EPS is supporting considering EPS CAGR of 26%
(FY26-28E) and expected multiple re-rating led by changing business mix. Buy
.
 Operational beat: Q4FY26 EBITDA of Rs 37.9bn (+43%YoY) was ~15% ahead of
our estimates. Revenues grew by +17%YoY to Rs 343bn led by strong all-round
performance, integration of Atsumitec and FX tailwinds. Adj. PAT came in at
Rs 14.7bn (+40%YoY), underpinned by strong operational performance.
 Strong non-auto business outlook: Management continued to express
optimism around emerging business momentum. Aerospace order book grew by
+20% YoY to US$1.8bn (India aerospace order book now exceeds EU, reflecting
strong growth momentum) while consumer electronics to witness robust growth
post 3rd plant SOP from Q3FY27 onwards.
 Reiterate BUY: We increase our EBITDA estimates by 7% to bake in improved
outlook and margin performance. Our EPS estimates are raised by ~3-5% due
to higher depreciation and increased tax rate. Thus, we arrive at a TP of
Rs 160 (from Rs 145). Re-iterate BUY.

emails.text_body:
---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Simran Thakkar <simran@beascapital.in>
Date: Thu, May 21, 2026 at 8:11 PM
Subject: Fw: [JMFL] Honeywell Automation India | EBITDA margin recovers;
upgrade to BUY
To: Chiraag Kapil <ceekay@muns.io>

Get Outlook for Android <https://aka.ms/AAb9ysg>
------------------------------
*From:* Priyankar Biswas <Priyankar.Biswas@jmfl.com>
*Sent:* Thursday, May 21, 2026 7:25:04 AM
*To:* Simran Thakkar <simran@beascapital.in>
*Subject:* [JMFL] Honeywell Automation India | EBITDA margin recovers;
upgrade to BUY

Hi Simran,

India | Industrials | Rating Upgrade

Honeywell Automation India | EBITDA margin recovers; upgrade to BUY

HWA IN | BUY | Price Target: INR 44,000 | Last Close: INR 30,180

HWA’s Q4FY26 results were largely on expected lines with gross/EBITDA
margins in line with our estimates. The recovery in gross margin (40%+) and
moderation of other expenses potentially signal that execution of loss
orders and the cycle of their associated provisioning may be behind. We
expect gross margin to be stable with Q4FY26 levels over FY27/28E and, with
reduced provisioning, expect EBITDA margin to sustain at 15–16% over
FY27–28E. If the management’s ‘razor and razor blade’ approach succeeds
then EBITDA margin could potentially rise to 18–20%. Given the sharp
correction (19% in 12M versus 4% fall in Nifty) we view risk-reward as
significantly favourable and upgrade to BUY with TP of INR 44,000 (50x
FY28E EPS of INR 880). Trading at 40/34x FY27/28E EPS, the stock is
significantly lagging valuation of peers (ABB/SIEM).

Results likely affirm that execution of low-margin (loss) orders may be
behind: HWA’s sales at INR 11.81bn (+6% YoY, +1% QoQ) was marginally below
our estimates. Gross margin at 41% was in line, but it is a significant
140bp YoY increase, unlike in automation peers like ABB where component
inflation impacted margins. Similarly, EBITDA margin at 15.7% (+135bps YoY)
is in line with other expenses now at 7.9% of sales versus 9.3% in Q4FY26,
potentially highlighting lower loss order provisioning. This may
potentially indicate legacy contracts have been largely executed and
recurring margin impact over FY25/26 is largely behind. Importantly, OCF
rose 16% YoY to INR 4.93bn (highest ever), translating into a record 81%
conversion of EBITDA to OCF.

‘Razor and razor blade’ approach may lead to strong long-term margins:
Globally, the parent entity had adopted a ‘razor and razor blade’ approach
that had led to margin expansion. This approach involves taking
lower-margin project orders involving systems integration leading to higher
product sales but lower margins initially. In the long term, services
revenue rises driven by software (Forge)-led monetisation with high
long-term EBITDA margin (20%+). Thus, as projects are delivered, eventually
services increase, which can potentially lead to 18–20% long-term steady
state margin (versus our estimate of 15–16% over FY27–28E). Globally, for
the parent, Business Automation (BA) and Industrial Automation (IA)
segments delivered 25.7% and 19.5% EBITDA margins, respectively, in CY24,
helped by large scale of the deployed product.

Completion of large projects should support services growth; exports may
remain robust: Completion of marquee projects like aircraft ground lighting
at Noida airport and others can drive services revenue recognition from
FY27. Further, HWA remains strong on Middle East exports where we
anticipate continue strength in capex in oil & gas, renewables,
electrolysers, transmission equipment and data centres. The sharp
depreciation of INR versus USD will also aid HWA since exports accounted
for 40–42% of sales over FY23–25. Services and exports are higher margin
and, thus, we do see potential for margin upside even on our current
estimates.

Trades at 34x FY28 P/E (lowest among automation peers); upgrade to BUY with
TP of INR 44,000 as risk reward is strongly favourable: HWA trades
significantly below its long-term (10Y) trading range of 40x–60x largely
reflective of the margin weakness and, importantly, lack of management
communication on issues over the last few years. However, we believe
margins have turned the corner based on Q4FY26 results and with quarterly
EPS at INR 180+ (4Q) we gain visibility of INR 750+ of EPS for FY27E
(leading to 11% rise in EPS). We, however, broadly retain FY28 estimates.
We value HWA at 50x (long-term average) FY28E EPS of INR 880 to derive our
TP of INR 44,000 and upgrade to BUY.

*CLICK HERE TO READ OUR DETAILED REPORT.
<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21563&DownloadType=show>*

Would be glad to address any queries that you may have.
Best regards
Priyankar Biswas
Executive Director | Institutional Equity Research

*From:* Ritesh Shah <ritesh.shah@investec.com>
*Sent:* Thursday, May 21, 2026 7:41:47 AM

*Subject:* Astral Ltd: In line Q4 - Buy

[image: Image]

Astral Ltd (ASTL.NS)

Price: INR1,445 | Target: INR1,710 | Rec: Buy

Astral reported in-line EBITDA with an improvement in working capital and
cash-flow conversion. Management articulated a long-term roadmap that
underscores a gradual shift from PVC to PP and the company’s readiness for
this transition. In the near term, key growth drivers include benefits from
CPVC backward integration, gains from decentralizing operations (with lead
distances under 400km), deeper distribution reach, and ongoing technology
initiatives.  For FY27, mgmt. guides 10-15% vol. growth for piping,
15-20/25% revenue growth for adhesives/Bathware-Paints. With a weaker INR,
reinstatement of PVC import duties from 1 July 2026, and higher freight
costs, we see a high likelihood of PVC prices moving up, which should
mitigate the risk of inventory losses. We bake in Q4, tweak estimates.
Reiterate BUY.
 Q4FY26: First take: In-line
<https://securities.investec.com/research/api/document/FT_492290/W/pdf?access_token=f86bf693d74000b90d18a07718df7032>
 KTA: 1. Pipes: a) CPVC backward integration (40kt, Rs1.2b) to eliminate
import dependence; full benefit from FY28 as it plays on CF fungibility,
aiding market share gains. Initial capacity to suffice 50-60% of captive
requirement; doubling capacity to take modest Rs150m capex with <12months
timeline.  b) New products & SKU expansion: O-PVC, PP-R, ElectroFusion
Fittings to cater gas, industrial, and STP segments; SKUs increased from
6,939 to 8,103. c) Decentralization + Bathware add growth levers —
Kanpur/Hyderabad plants improve logistics; mgmt. remains comfortable on
outbound lead and may look to strategically add a few more plants; FY27
volume guidance 10–15%. 2. Adhesives a) Distribution scale-up is the
primary growth driver — present in 13,000 towns, targeting 20,000; rural
penetration via "New Bharat" deepens reach in tier 3/4 towns. b) Brand
investment shifts model from trade-push to consumer-pull — Ranbir Kapoor as
Bondtite ambassador builds pricing power; FY27 growth target of 15–20% vs.
industry 8–10%.
c) Trubuild relaunch + Bond It UK/US add optionality — complete
waterproofing portfolio repositioned; post-restructuring UK business
expected to improve profitability; US new launches add upside. 3 Paints
targets EBITDA profitability in FY27 with 25% revenue growth. 4. Bathware
targeting 25% growth in FY27; MEA presence being built across modern trade
and e-commerce.
 BUY: We bake in EBITDA/PAT CAGR at 17/25% over FY26-28E, as we retain our
conservative forecasts across segments, factoring weak local macro,
potential demand risk on price elasticity of demand. Timely execution on
CPVC backward integration poses upside risk to our forecasts. We continue
to prefer ASTRA over SI.

*From:* Nuvama Wealth Research <researchwealth@nuvama.com>
*Sent:* Tuesday, May 19, 2026 8:41:27 AM
*To:* Simran Thakkar <simran@beascapital.in>
*Subject:* Pricol - Q4FY26 Result Update

*Pricol **(CMP INR554; Mcap INR6,751cr; Target INR660) *

*Pricol (PRICOL) reported a strong set of earnings in Q4FY26, with a
**43%/62%/110%
YoY growth in consolidated sales/EBITDA/PAT. The organic standalone
business recorded strong sales growth outpacing 2W industry production
growth. EBITDA margin expansion was driven by a higher gross margin and
lower staff expenses but was partially offset by a rise in other expenses.
Geopolitical concerns, elevated commodity prices, and higher freight costs
may exert pressure on demand and the overall cost structure for the
industry in the near term. Over the next three years, the management aims
to maintain its market share in the instrument cluster segment, aided by
confirmed Letters of Intent (LoIs) for most programmes. It seeks to double
turnover in its P3L business driven by business wins and capacity
additions.* *We retain **‘BUY’ but lower **our TP marginally to INR660 from
INR680 earlier, implying ~19% upside. *

*Strong earnings growth backed by margin expansion*

*Consolidated sales, *up 43% YoY to INR1,099cr, include consolidation of
the Pricol Precision Products Pvt (INR240cr) acquired in December 2024.
Standalone sales rose 38% YoY organically, outperforming 2W production
growth by more than 1,700bp. *Consolidated EBITDA * rose 62% YoY to
INR143cr, with EBITDA margin expanding 156bp YoY to 13% on a higher gross
margin and lower employee expenses but was partially offset by a rise in
other expenses. *Consolidated* *PAT* surged 110% YoY on greater operational
efficiency and a lower tax rate. Consolidated revenue/ EBITDA/PAT grew
50%/48%/50% YoY to INR4,041cr/INR493cr/INR251cr in FY26.

*Near term outlook cautious amid geopolitical headwinds*

The management is cautious in the near term due to the geopolitical
situation in West Asia, which has led to sharp currency volatility and
elevated commodity and freight costs. Prices of
polymers/aluminium/semiconductors/memory control devices have surged by
~55%/~62%/ ~35%/~28%. While it has initiated supplementary pricing
discussions with OEMs and is working towards recovering the costs, the
management indicated that the entire cost increase is unlikely to be passed
on to customers. As a result, some softening in earnings is expected over
the next few quarters. Despite these near-term challenges, it reiterated
its commitment to invest in R&D, technology, and growth initiatives without
scaling back on strategic capex.

*Leadership in instrument clusters and premiumisation opportunities stay
intact*

PRICOL is confident of retaining its market share in instrument clusters
over the next three years. It has maintained/grown its market share by
volume/value aided by confirmed LoIs for most ongoing programmes and
investments in R&D. The premiumisation trend in 2Ws and passenger vehicles
is driving demand. In PVs, it has majorly expanded its presence with Tata
Motors, where 75–80% of the latter’s vehicles now use its instrument
clusters. It seeks to raise its wallet share with key clients and expects
content per vehicle to rise by at least 50% over the next three years.

*Valuation and view *

Despite a strong Q4FY26, the management is cautious in the near term. This
led to a correction in the stock which we view as a good entry point for a
medium-term investment. We are positive on its medium-term story given the
rising digitisation and premiumisation in the 2W space and rising share of
business with PV OEMs, with a focus on client and product additions over
the next few years. On a one-year forward P/E, the stock is trading at
26.9x, 19% below its last two years’ average (~33x). Our SoTP valuation
leads to a TP of INR660, implying 26.4x/20.7x FY27/FY28 P/E.

--

Research Summary

*Kerala Market Gains Traction, IMFL Proprietary Volume Grows by 39% YoY*

Incremental market share gains (+1.5% in FY26) have boosted growth for
proprietary brands across categories such as whiskey and brandy. AAB
reported  a growth of 33% in IMFL Proprietary segment for FY26. However,
overall topline declined by ~5% because of transition of contracted
manufacturing from full accounting to revenue-only accounting. Further,
oversupply of ethanol led to lower offtake, causing a decline in ethanol
volume by ~17% for FY26. Key monitorables include ramp-up in premium
product launches, scaling up of proprietary brands across new markets,
recovery in ethanol demand driven by policy support and margin sustenance.

*View and Valuation*

We *reduce our FY27E revenue estimate by ~5% to factor in continued
weakness in the ethanol business *amid industry-wide oversupply and lower
blending allocation. However, we remain constructive on AAB, supported
by a *strong
traction in proprietary IMFL brands, RTD and single malt launches.* *We
continue to factor in ~36% growth in the IMFL proprietary portfolio* and
marginally increase our EBITDA margin estimate driven by better product mix
and operating efficiency. We expect a Revenue / EBITDA / PAT CAGR of 9.8% /
14.9% / 16.1% over FY26–FY29E, respectively. Thus, we *maintain our ‘BUY’
rating with a revised target price of INR 1,070 (vs. 1,110) *using the DCF
methodology. Our TP implies a PE of 18.5x on FY28E EPS.

34.5% YoY, aided by higher value-added services and pricing in new
contracts, operational efficiencies, and an increase in self-managed
centres. Net revenue/application improved to INR3,302 from INR2,903 YoY.

Visa application volumes grew a healthy 9.8% YoY to 10.8lk in Q4FY26. In
FY26, volumes rose to 44.1lk from 37.5lk YoY. In Q4FY26, revenue from this
segment grew 7% YoY to INR472cr, with EBITDA margin expanding to 38.1% from
34.2% YoY.

The digital services segment continued to scale, with revenue more than
doubling to INR1,158cr in FY26. In Q4FY26, revenue grew 36% YoY to INR343cr
driven by business correspondent (BC) operations, loan distribution,
consolidation of Aadifidelis Solutions, and assisted digital services.
EBITDA margin was subdued due to the low-margin Aadifidelis Solutions
business, which operates at 4–5%. The management clarified that margin
pressure is driven by the business mix rather than operational weakness.

The INR2,055cr UIDAI Aadhaar Seva Kendra (ASK) project has entered the
rollout phase, with 40–50 centres operational. The management expects
deployment of over 300 centres over the next 12–18 months, with EBITDA
margin expected in the 15–20% range over the contract tenure.

   - In FY26, the company secured several strategic contract wins including:
   - The five-year Slovakia global visa outsourcing contract;
   - MEA India visa centre contract in China;
   - Cyprus visa outsourcing mandates;
   - Renewal of attestation and apostille services with MEA India; and
   - Bihar Aadhaar PEC project worth INR100cr.

To strengthen its inorganic growth strategy and digital and financial
inclusion capabilities, it aims to utilise INR138cr from its IPO proceeds
to acquire Atyati Technologies. The management said that while geopolitical
disruptions may temporarily impact travel demand, its diversified presence
across more than 80 countries and 46 client governments helps offset
region-specific volatility.

BLSIN strengthened its AI and technology capabilities through AI-powered
visa modernisation initiatives, AI voice bots, analytics, and Cloud-based
infrastructure aimed at improving operational efficiency and scalability.

*Valuation and view*

Strong growth momentum across its core visa and consular business
continues, aided by healthy growth in visa applications, improving
realisations, increasing self-managed centres, and better pricing in newly
won contracts. It benefits from a strong global tender pipeline, expanding
geographic presence, and rising contribution from value-added services,
which have supported healthy margin expansion in FY26. The ramp-up of the
INR2,055cr UIDAI Aadhaar Seva Kendra project and scaling of digital
services provide strong medium-term revenue visibility.

While margin in the digital services business is subdued due to the
low-margin Aadifidelis Solutions-led business mix, strong cash generation,
a net cash position of INR1,434cr, ongoing AI-led operational initiatives,
and strategic investments such as the proposed acquisition of Atyati
Technologies strengthen its long-term growth outlook. We maintain ‘BUY’
with a SoTP-based TP of INR431.

--

Research Summary

*Strong execution drives record quarterly profitability growth: * SANSERA
delivered a strong Q4FY26 performance wherein the revenue increased 28%
YoY, while EBITDA/PAT grew 52%/108% YoY, respectively. EBITDA margin
expanded sharply by 306 bps YoY to 19.3%, supported by favourable product
mix, operating leverage and rising contribution from high-margin ADS and
semiconductor businesses. FY26 revenue increased 16% YoY, while PAT grew
51% YoY, reflecting healthy execution across core auto and emerging
businesses. *We believe SANSERA’s transition, from a traditional
ICE-focussed supplier towards a diversified engineering solutions company,
continues to gain momentum. Reflecting strong progress on diversification,
non-auto and xEV businesses accounted for nearly 30–32% of revenue in FY26
as compared to 16% in FY21.*

*ADS business accelerates diversification journey: *ADS continues to be
SANSERA’s key long-term growth driver; FY26 revenue surged 155% YoY to INR
3,155 Mn, led by strong aerospace and semiconductor demand. The ADS order
backlog remained robust at INR 44.6 Bn, while the management guided FY27E
ADS revenue at INR 5.5–6.0 Bn, indicating a strong growth visibility*. We
believe increasing localisation, AI-led semiconductor opportunities, rising
global aerospace outsourcing and expansion into complex high-value
structural components strengthen SANSERA’s positioning in precision
engineering.* Further, the Nichidai Corporation JV, ongoing ADS capacity
expansion and healthy cash balance of INR 3,972 Mn provide additional
growth visibility and margin support in the medium term.

*View and Valuation:* We revise our FY27E/28E EPS estimate upwards by
10.7%/18.0%, factoring in a strong order book and an improved growth
outlook. We value the company at 35x on FY28E EPS, arriving at a target
price of *INR 2,950*. We upgrade our rating from *‘REDUCE’ to ‘ADD’*, given
the diversification into the fast-growing and high margin ADS segment, with
a robust INR 44.6 Bn order backlog and INR 2.5 Bn capex driving profitable
non-auto diversification.

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Simran Thakkar <simran@beascapital.in>
Date: Thu, May 21, 2026 at 8:07 PM
Subject: Fw: Apollo Hospitals – Strong execution continues; 24/7 nearing
break-even – BUY
To: Chiraag Kapil <ceekay@muns.io>

Get Outlook for Android <https://aka.ms/AAb9ysg>
------------------------------
*From:* Rahul Jeewani, IIFLCAP <rahul.jeewani@iiflcap.com>
*Sent:* Thursday, May 21, 2026 5:39:23 PM
*To:* Simran Thakkar <simran@beascapital.in>
*Subject:* Apollo Hospitals – Strong execution continues; 24/7 nearing
break-even – BUY

Hi Simran,

*FY26 was yet another solid year for Apollo, with overall Rev/Ebitda growth
of 16/25%, driven by 13/19/20% revenue growth in its Hospital/HealthCo/AHLL
business. While Hospital and Offline Pharmacy Ebitda margins were steady at
~24.4% and ~7.7% resp.,   Apollo 24/7 Ebitda losses also declined from
Rs4.5bn in FY25 to Rs2.5bn in FY26. Looking ahead, Hospital revenue from
existing capacities is expected to clock 13-14% Cagr driven by improvement
in occupancies, while additional 3-4% growth should come from ~20% increase
in bed capacities (1,600 new beds) over FY26/27. Although new hospitals
will likely drag Hospital Ebitda margins by ~120bps in FY27ii, we expect
margins to normalise to ~25% by FY29ii driving 17% Hospital Ebitda Cagr
over FY26-29ii. With 24/7 expected to achieve cash/reported Ebitda
break-even (BE) in 1Q/ 3QFY27, mgmt reiterated its target of achieving
annualised revenue run-rate of Rs250bn for HealthCo with 6.5-7% Ebitda
margins by 4QFY27 (vs 4.8% in FY26). Robust high-teens revenue growth
across segments and BE for 24/7 will drive 22% overall Ebitda Cagr (18%
ex-24/7) over FY26-29ii. BUY. TP Rs9,700 (16% upside).*

*We forecast 16/17% Rev/Ebitda Cagr for the Hospitals over FY26-29ii,*
driven by occupancy ramp-up in existing hospitals (from 67-68%) and ~20%
incremental bed additions over FY26-27. Four new hospitals (Pune, Kolkata,
Hyderabad, Delhi) dragged Hospital Ebitda margins by ~160bps in 4Q,
excluding which margins would have been 25.5%. Factoring in a ~100-150bps
drag from new hospitals, we expect Hospital margins to decline to 23.2% in
FY27ii (24.4% FY26), before normalising to 24/25% by FY28/29ii.

*Apollo 24/7 Ebitda losses moderated 42% QoQ in 4Q and 44% YoY in FY26,*
driven by adj. Platform GMV growth of 24% in FY26. With ramp-up in the
Insurance broking business, we forecast Platform GMV to clock 17% Cagr over
FY26-29ii and Rev/GMV ratio to improve from 63% to 65% over the next 3
years. We build-in reported Ebitda BE for 24/7 in 3QFY27, forecast 24/7
losses to moderate from Rs2.5bn in FY26 to Rs180m in FY27ii, and expect an
Ebitda profit of Rs900m/1.5bn in FY28/29ii.

*We bake-in 20% revenue Cagr for HealthCo (+ Keimed) over FY26-29ii* and
expect HealthCo Ebitda margins to improve from 4.8% (6.6% ex-24/7) in FY26
to 6.2/6.7/7.0% over FY27-29ii (vs guidance of 6.5-7% for 4QFY27) led by BE
for 24/7, reduction in ESOP costs from Rs1.2bn in FY26 to Rs500m in FY27ii,
and ~50bps margin expansion in Keimed.

Please follow the link below to our report:
IIFL - Apollo Hospitals - 4QFY26 review - 20260521.pdf
<https://www.iiflcap.com/researchpack/ResearchDownload.aspx?Pid=p6Wxkal2RP0%3d&Cid=nBo5af0GYS0%3d&fName=yccpIfvRUchHtQj4GDS3hbUVDpku%2bPNrjVsAn8rgGuVx8SD4RHOqnyykRIc6g9NXf%2b9HZrYSnnQ%3d&typ=APSSeVhmQ%2b8%3d>

Best Regards,

Rahul Jeewani, research analyst

*To:* Rahul Mehta <rahul@beascapital.in>
*Cc:* aniket.singh2@kotak.com <aniket.singh2@kotak.com>;
samitinjoy.basak@kotak.com <samitinjoy.basak@kotak.com>
*Subject:* [Kotak] KIMS (CMP: Rs717, FV: Rs695, REDUCE): Still not there yet

* ------------------------------ *

Please click *here*
<https://kie.kotak.com/Kinsite/MailQueue/DownloadPdf/?code=f2f26085-d351-4cf0-9680-7e5409d35f25>
for the full report.
*Still not there yet*
KIMS delivered a ~5% operating miss in 4QFY26, led by higher-than-expected
losses in new hospitals due to continued delays in insurance empanelment.
Even as core clusters exhibit steady growth and margins, the ongoing
expansion across Maharashtra, Karnataka and Kerala continues to weigh on
profitability. We expect the gradual ramp-up of new units, alongside stable
performance in AP/Telangana, to support earnings recovery hereon and build
in a robust ~29% EBITDA CAGR over FY2026-29E. After the recent stock move,
we believe the earnings recovery is factored in and hence, downgrade KIMS
to REDUCE, with an unchanged FV of Rs695.
*Higher losses at new hospitals drive operating miss in 4QFY26*
KIMS’ 4QFY26 sales grew 35% yoy to Rs10.7 bn (+8% qoq), largely in line
with expectations. However, EBITDA at Rs2.1 bn (+4% yoy, +4% qoq) was 5%
below estimates, with margins declining to 19.2% ((-)560 bps yoy). The
EBITDA miss was driven by losses in new hospitals (~Rs320 mn in 4QFY26),
even as mature hospitals sustained healthy profitability with ~29% margin,
highlighting the underlying strength of the core business. Akin to the past
seven quarters, ARPOB growth was robust at ~14% yoy, led by 14% yoy ARPP
growth. After a strong ~18% CAGR over FY2024-26, we expect higher
cash/insurance mix, the addition of new specialties and an entry into
higher realization markets to drive a ~6% ARPOB CAGR over FY2026-29E, which
we factor in. Despite the flux in margins after the commissioning of three
big hospitals (one in Thane and two in Bengaluru) in FY2026, we expect KIMS
to turn around the initial losses and report a 29% EBITDA CAGR over
FY2026-29E.

*Delay in insurance empanelment at new units; further expansion in the
offing*
KIMS’ bed addition plans remain largely on track. While KIMS has been
facing delays in insurance empanelment across new units, leading to a
lower-than-expected uptick, it continues to focus on scaling up recently
commissioned hospitals over the next 12-24 months. Compared with cumulative
EBITDA losses of ~Rs1.5 bn in FY2026, we factor in a positive EBITDA of
~Rs1.4 bn in FY2028E for four key new units (Nashik, Thane, Mahadevapura
and Electronic City). It aims to breakeven in these key assets over the
next few quarters. However, we highlight that the path to profitability
hinges on successful empanelment with insurance players in these regions.
At the same time, growth in Telangana will be volume-driven, led by new bed
additions, while AP margins should remain stable. Moreover, KIMS is
planning a QIP (up to ~Rs15 bn) to reduce debt by ~Rs10 bn and create
headroom for future expansions while also exploring inorganic opportunities
within existing markets.

*Execution uptick baked in; downgrade to REDUCE (ADD earlier); unchanged FV*
Factoring in a slightly slower ramp-up for new units, we cut FY2027-28E
EBITDA estimates by 1-3% and roll forward to derive an FV of Rs695
(unchanged). While KIMS is well-poised to be a prominent multi-specialty
hospital chain, given its increasing scale after the recent stock
performance, we believe valuations capture the upside and future earnings
improvement. Accordingly, we downgrade to REDUCE.

Best regards,

Subject: JMFS Fundamental Research - Daily Financial Market Digest (24th April 2026)

Dear All,

JMFS Research
April 24th, 2026
Daily Financial Market Digest

Top Corporate News & sector developments

Lux Industries: Company's board has given in-principle approval to demerge the business into three distinct verticals (with Verticals A and C being listed separately) while the PKT family retains overall control following a family settlement that also revises brand licensing pacts. Positive

Inventurus Knowledge Solutions: The company has announced a major cross-border acquisition, signing a deal to buy US-based healthcare IT firm TruBridge for up to $565 million. Positive

Himadri Speciality Chemical: The company has successfully commenced operations at its new anode material production facility in West Bengal, which boasts an installed capacity of 200 MTPA. Positive

Gujarat Themis Biosyn: Company has signed an Asset Purchase Agreement with Sanofi to acquire 13 established branded generic products for €158 million in cash, expected to close by December 2026. The portfolio, present in 55+ countries across Europe, the Middle East and Africa, reported net sales of ~€62 million in 2025. It strengthens GTBL's global generics platform, expands its anti-infective presence and enables forward integration leveraging its existing API and fermentation capabilities.

Eternal: As per media reports Zomato has agreed to drop a contract term that penalized restaurants for offering cheaper meals to walk-in diners, after opposition from eateries who said the policy undermined their pricing decisions. The clause was never enforced but has been dropped now.

Force Motors: The automaker has executed an agreement to acquire Veera Tanneries for Rs 162 crore, turning it into a wholly-owned subsidiary. Positive

Waaree energies & Vikram Solar: The US Commerce Department has imposed preliminary anti-dumping duties on solar imports from India (123%), Laos (22.46%), and Indonesia (35.17%), following a petition by American solar manufacturers alleging unfair pricing. Combined with existing countervailing duties, total AD/CVD rates reach ~234% for India. Negative

GMR Airports: ADP (Paris airport operator) plans to sell up to a 7.3% stake in GMR Airports for ~€924 million ($1.08 billion). The deal begins with an immediate offload of a 3.4% stake for €256 million (~₹2,800 crore), with the remainder to follow.
4QFY26 Business Update

United Breweries: Heineken India saw net revenue grow low-single-digit. Total volume (including contract brewing now classified as licensed volume) grew mid-single-digit. Premium segment grew mid-teens, led by Kingfisher Ultra. Launched Kingfisher Smooth in mainstream segment.

Gujarat Gas: The company ensured an uninterrupted gas supply to the Morbi unit to support the revival of the local ceramic industry, providing price stability amid ongoing geopolitical disruptions that previously caused a surge in raw material costs. Gas consumption in Morbi has rebounded to ~2.70 Mmscmd in April after operations of several ceramic units were suspended in March, which had temporarily affected the livelihood of nearly 2 lakh workers.

Aditya Birla Real Estate (FY26): Birla Estates reported booking value of Rs 8,136 crore. Collections increased 23.5% year-on-year.
4QFY26 Result Update

Tata Capital: Company reported a healthy quarter with PAT growth at +47% YoY/+16% QoQ (despite missing estimate by 4.7%), and ~17bps/49bps increase in RoA/RoE at 2.1%/13%. This was driven by healthy AUM growth, improving asset quality and continued operating leverage. AUM grew +20% YoY / +6% QoQ (28% YoY ex-TMFL), in-line with their guidance, led by sustained traction in retail, corporate and housing finance. TCHFL remained a standout with +29% YoY AUM growth and best-in-class asset quality. However, at the overall level, NIM performance remained relatively softer than expected (-9bps QoQ, -15bps JMFe) as asset-side repricing and higher corporate mix continued to weigh on yields, leading to a miss in our PAT estimates. Credit cost improved sharply to 0.9% (0.8% ex-motor finance), while GS3/NS3 declined by 20bps/15bps QoQ. Strong Results

Infosys: Company reported 4QFY26 results – revenue and margins were below expectations. Revenues came in at USD 5,040mn, -1.2% QoQ/+6.6% YoY (estimates: USD 5,101mn). Revenues declined 1.3% cc QoQ vs estimates of -0.5% QoQ.  EBIT margin came in at 20.9%, down ~20bps QoQ, vs estimates of 21.2%. Margin tailwinds: currency (+40bps), realisations (+30bps), Margin headwinds: amortization impact (-50bps), one-off gain normalization (-30bps), employee costs (-20 bps). Reported PAT stood at Rs 85.1bn, versus JMFL estimates of Rs 77.7bn, PAT was supported by reversal of tax of Rs 7.7bn. Infy adr was down 4%. Weak Results

LTM: Company reported 4QFY26 results – revenues and margins were below versus expectations. Revenues came in at USD 1,222mn, +1.2% QoQ/+8.1% YoY (estimates: USD 1,227mn). Revenues grew 1.2% cc QoQ vs estimates of 1.6% QoQ. EBIT margin came in at 15.1%, down 100bps QoQ/ up 130bps YoY, vs estimates of 15.6%. This was largely due to partial wage hikes and productivity commitments in key accounts, partially offset by FX gains. Reported PAT stood at Rs 13.9bn, versus estimates of Rs 14.8bn. PAT was positively impacted by reversal of Rs 622mn in labour code impact. Weak Results

Cyient: Company reported mixed Q4 results, with revenue increasing 4.2% QoQ to ₹1,927 crore from ₹1,849 crore. However, profitability weakened as EBIT declined 6.8% QoQ to ₹156 crore from ₹167 crore, with EBIT margin contracting by 95 basis points to 8.08% from 9.03%. Net profit saw a sharp drop of 40.3% QoQ, falling to ₹54.8 crore compared to ₹91.8 crore in the previous quarter.

CIE Automotive India: Company reported a strong performance in Q4FY26, with revenue rising to Rs 2611.9 crore from Rs 2272.6 crore, marking a 14.9% YoY and 9.1% QoQ increase. EBITDA grew to Rs 401.9 crore from Rs 335.5 crore, up 19.8% YoY and 20.1% QoQ, while EBITDA margin improved to 15.4% compared to 14.7% YoY and 13.9% QoQ. Profit after tax also saw solid growth, reaching Rs 249.3 crore versus Rs 206.4 crore, reflecting a 20.8% YoY and 22.0% QoQ increase.
Corporate Action
Cyient Ltd: The company has announced a share buyback worth Rs 720 crore. The buyback will be executed at a fixed price of Rs 1,125 per share, which reflects a premium of nearly 20 per cent over the stock’s closing price. The company will carry out the entire process through the tender offer route
Rolex Rings: The company's Board has approved a buyback of up to 1 crore equity shares at ₹180 per share, totalling ₹180 crores. The shares, with a face value of ₹1 each, represent 3.67% of the existing paid-up equity capital. The buyback will be conducted via the tender offer route, open to all equity shareholders on a proportionate basis as of a record date to be announced.

Company/Sector Update

Infosys | Soft 4QFY26; Healthy large deal wins<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21219&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              BUY                 INR 1,500

Infosys reported 4QFY26 results – revenue and margins were below expectations. Revenue declined 1.3% cc QoQ vs expectation of 0.5% cc QoQ decline. EBIT margins at 20.9% – missed JMFL and consensus expectations. Forward looking indicators – i) Infosys guided for 1.5%-3.5% YoY cc revenue growth for FY27 (includes 20-25bps of acquisition), lower than our expectation of 2% to 4%, margin guidance was in-line at 20-22%. ii) LTM large deal TCV grew ~28% YoY in FY26 versus decline of ~34% in FY25 (LTM book to bill of 0.74x in FY26 versus 0.60x in FY25). ii) Headcount declined 2.6% QoQ / up 1.6% YoY. iii) Management commentary – Lower end of guidance factors demand deterioration while top end factors demand improvement. Margin headwinds for FY27 also includes a potential 70bps dilutive impact from acquisitions. Prefer Infosys among the top 6 Indian IT companies given healthy large deal wins. Valuations are at ~16x FY27 consensus EPS. We revise our EPS estimates down by 1-2% over FY27-28E, incorporating 4Q results. Maintain target multiple of 19x Sep 27 – revised TP of INR 1,500 (versus INR 1525 earlier). Maintain BUY.

Tata Capital | Strong growth and asset quality trends; miss on NIM<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21220&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              ADD                 INR 380

Tata Capital reported a healthy quarter with PAT growth at +47% YoY/+16% QoQ (despite missing JMFe by 4.7%), and ~17bps/49bps increase in RoA/RoE at 2.1%/13%. This was driven by healthy AUM growth, improving asset quality and continued operating leverage. AUM grew +20% YoY / +6% QoQ (28% YoY ex-TMFL), in-line with their guidance, led by sustained traction in retail, corporate and housing finance. TCHFL remained a standout with +29% YoY AUM growth and best-in-class asset quality. However, at the overall level, NIM performance remained relatively softer than expected (-9bps QoQ, -15bps JMFe) as asset-side repricing and higher corporate mix continued to weigh on yields, leading to a miss in our PAT estimates. Credit cost improved sharply to 0.9% (0.8% ex-motor finance), while GS3/NS3 declined by 20bps/15bps QoQ. Motor finance also showed improving profitability. Management remains confident of delivering FY28 guidance of ~2.5–2.7% RoA (% of net loans) and >30% PAT CAGR. We believe improving earnings quality and motor finance normalisation support premium valuations. We build in EPS CAGR of ~34% during FY26-28E with average RoA (% of avg. assets)/RoE of ~2.2%/15% in FY27/28E (BAF/CIFC- EPS CAGR: ~28%/26%, RoA: ~4%/2.6%; RoE: ~21%/20% during the same period). We maintain ADD with revised TP of INR380, valuing it at ~2.7/18x FY28 BVPS/EPS (vs. ~3.8/19x for BAF/ ~3.1/18x for CIFC).

LTM | Soft 4QFY26; valuation remains elevated<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21218&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              REDUCE                       INR 4,115

LTM reported 4QFY26 results – revenues and margins were below versus expectations. Revenue growth came in at 1.2% cc QoQ versus expectation of 1.6% cc QoQ. EBIT margins at 15.1% versus JMFL/consensus expectations of 15.6%/15.5%. Forward looking indicators – i) LTM order inflow grew 10.2% YoY in FY26 versus 7% YoY growth in FY25 (LTM book to bill of 1.4x in FY26 versus 1.3x in FY25). ii) Headcount remained stable on a sequential basis (+4.3% YoY). iii) Management commentary – confident to continue the growth momentum in FY27. However, due to certain macroeconomic environment that might happen within the three months period, there could be some softness in a particular quarter. Valuations are at ~21x FY27 consensus EPS. LTM is at ~7% premium to Mphasis for a similar growth profile in FY27, as per consensus. We reduce our EPS estimates by 3-5% over FY27-28E, incorporating 4Q results. Maintain target multiple of 19x Sep 27 – revised TP of INR 4,115 (versus INR 4,285 earlier). Maintain REDUCE.

UTI AMC | Reports loss due to other income; all is well<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21217&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              ADD                 INR 1,100

UTI AMC reported a loss of INR 514mn in 4Q only due to MTM losses resulting in negative other income of INR 1,630mn. Operating performance was sound; revenue fell just 1% QoQ while QAAUM was down 3%. Employee expenses were back to the trend rate of INR 1.3bn while other expenses were elevated with marketing efforts. In the concall, the CEO (who took over w.e.f. Feb’26) highlighted the focus on building an agile salesforce and a product suite that can deliver across market cycles to ultimately deliver “growth”. We believe we should look out in this quarter for the structural rejuvenation of India’s oldest AMC under new leadership and a vibrant team. We raise our TP slightly to INR 1,100 (from INR 1,050 earlier), valuing the AMC at 14x FY27E EPS of INR 78 (against 13x FY28E EPS of INR 82 earlier). We maintain ADD.

Indian Energy Exchange | 4QFY26 first cut: In-line; coupling overhang continues<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21216&DownloadType=show>

Flash Update

IEX reported in-line results, with trading volumes growing 24% YoY. FY26 market clearing prices in the DAM (-14% YoY) and RTM (-16% YoY) declined due to subdued power demand and increase in supply side liquidity. Key highlights from the 4QFY26 results are as follows- Revenue increased 23% YoY to INR 1.7 bn (-3% vs. JMFe / +3% consensus), driven by 24% YoY growth in trading volumes.- EBITDA margin reported at 85.7% vs. 85.3% / 86.1% in 4QFY25 / JMFe.- PAT came at INR 1.3 bn (11% YoY / -9% JMFe / -4% Cons), due to lower than expected other income (-32% YoY / -41% JMFe).- Market clearing prices in the DAM (Day-Ahead Market) and RTM (Real-Time Market) stood at INR 3.86 per unit and INR 3.59 per unit, down 14% and 16% YoY due to increased supply liquidity on the exchange.- The Board of Directors announced a final dividend of INR 2.0 per share.

Tips Music | Strong end to FY26; steady guidance for FY27<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21214&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              ADD                 INR 730

Tips Music reported strong 4QFY26 earnings growth of 92.9% YoY, driven by 32.4% revenue growth (19.1%/10.7%/21.4% in 1Q/2Q/3QFY26) and ~2,660bps EBIT margin expansion. Top-line growth was supported by sustained traction in the company’s catalogue, particularly its 90s repertoire, with management reiterating that growth was entirely recurring with no one-offs. Paid subscription contribution has inched up to 10–15% of digital revenue (10–12% last year). Margin expansion was largely led by lower content spends (down 54.6% YoY) due to deferment of a key movie-based music content release to 1QFY27. We broadly factor in management’s revenue guidance of ~20% revenue growth in FY27 (up from 15% expected earlier) but build content spends at the lower end of the guidance of INR 800mn-900mn basis historical under-delivery. Overall, we raise FY27E–28E EPS by 12–15%. We roll-forward to Mar’27 TP for a revised TP of INR 730 (INR 580 earlier) as we now value the stock at 32x P/E versus 30x earlier.

Bharat Electronics | Order momentum strong; valuations expensive<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21213&DownloadType=show>

Company Update                     ADD                 INR 485

Bharat Electronics (BEL) is well placed to benefit from rising capex in India’s defence electronics. In our view, it could be a key beneficiary of electronic components, avionics and radar orders across large defence platforms. The company has secured order inflows of INR 300bn in FY26 (+60% YoY) ahead of its guidance of INR 270bn. We expect this ordering momentum to persist in FY27 too, with strong prospects of over INR 500bn. We forecast revenue/EPS CAGR of 16% each over FY26–28E. However, BEL’s valuation discount to capital goods growth stocks is close to an all-time low (discount at 8% versus 10-year average of 30%). In our view, the current stock price largely reflects these positives. We value BEL at 44x FY28 EPS of INR 11, arriving at our revised TP of INR 485 and assume coverage with an ADD rating.

Bharat Heavy Electricals | Journey of growth and profit begins<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21221&DownloadType=show>

Company Update                     BUY                 INR 393

BHEL has run up 32% in just one month versus 20%/19% for S&P BSE Industrials/Power index. We believe order book has not yet peaked given 18/14GW of pipeline along with improving non-thermal visibility. With the commissioning of 8.9/10GW of legacy projects, pickup in both execution and margin is certain in our view. Moreover, we reckon the permission to import from China, change in product mix in non-thermal orders (HVDC, naval guns, nuclear), indigenisation of supercritical and op-lev would propel margins to mid-teens over next two–three years. Easing of working capital stress is also likely considering large customer advances and commissioning of legacy projects. The stock is trading at 27x FY28E EPS (consensus) at Q4FY26E EBITDA margin of 11% (consensus). We anticipate a beat on margins, making it worthy of an earnings upgrade and re-rating as revenues compound 20% over FY25–28E while EBITDA margin expands from 4.4% to 11.2% in base case and RoE improves to at least 15% by FY28E (versus 25–30% over 2008–12 cycle). These factors call for a premium in our view; accordingly, we value the stock at a P/E of 32x (up from 30x), yielding a revised TP of INR 393 (INR 345 earlier); reiterate ‘BUY’.

Company/Sector Update

Infosys | Soft 4QFY26; Healthy large deal wins<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21219&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              BUY                 INR 1,500

Infosys reported 4QFY26 results – revenue and margins were below expectations. Revenue declined 1.3% cc QoQ vs expectation of 0.5% cc QoQ decline. EBIT margins at 20.9% – missed JMFL and consensus expectations. Forward looking indicators – i) Infosys guided for 1.5%-3.5% YoY cc revenue growth for FY27 (includes 20-25bps of acquisition), lower than our expectation of 2% to 4%, margin guidance was in-line at 20-22%. ii) LTM large deal TCV grew ~28% YoY in FY26 versus decline of ~34% in FY25 (LTM book to bill of 0.74x in FY26 versus 0.60x in FY25). ii) Headcount declined 2.6% QoQ / up 1.6% YoY. iii) Management commentary – Lower end of guidance factors demand deterioration while top end factors demand improvement. Margin headwinds for FY27 also includes a potential 70bps dilutive impact from acquisitions. Prefer Infosys among the top 6 Indian IT companies given healthy large deal wins. Valuations are at ~16x FY27 consensus EPS. We revise our EPS estimates down by 1-2% over FY27-28E, incorporating 4Q results. Maintain target multiple of 19x Sep 27 – revised TP of INR 1,500 (versus INR 1525 earlier). Maintain BUY.

Tata Capital | Strong growth and asset quality trends; miss on NIM<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21220&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              ADD                 INR 380

Tata Capital reported a healthy quarter with PAT growth at +47% YoY/+16% QoQ (despite missing JMFe by 4.7%), and ~17bps/49bps increase in RoA/RoE at 2.1%/13%. This was driven by healthy AUM growth, improving asset quality and continued operating leverage. AUM grew +20% YoY / +6% QoQ (28% YoY ex-TMFL), in-line with their guidance, led by sustained traction in retail, corporate and housing finance. TCHFL remained a standout with +29% YoY AUM growth and best-in-class asset quality. However, at the overall level, NIM performance remained relatively softer than expected (-9bps QoQ, -15bps JMFe) as asset-side repricing and higher corporate mix continued to weigh on yields, leading to a miss in our PAT estimates. Credit cost improved sharply to 0.9% (0.8% ex-motor finance), while GS3/NS3 declined by 20bps/15bps QoQ. Motor finance also showed improving profitability. Management remains confident of delivering FY28 guidance of ~2.5–2.7% RoA (% of net loans) and >30% PAT CAGR. We believe improving earnings quality and motor finance normalisation support premium valuations. We build in EPS CAGR of ~34% during FY26-28E with average RoA (% of avg. assets)/RoE of ~2.2%/15% in FY27/28E (BAF/CIFC- EPS CAGR: ~28%/26%, RoA: ~4%/2.6%; RoE: ~21%/20% during the same period). We maintain ADD with revised TP of INR380, valuing it at ~2.7/18x FY28 BVPS/EPS (vs. ~3.8/19x for BAF/ ~3.1/18x for CIFC).

LTM | Soft 4QFY26; valuation remains elevated<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21218&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              REDUCE                       INR 4,115

LTM reported 4QFY26 results – revenues and margins were below versus expectations. Revenue growth came in at 1.2% cc QoQ versus expectation of 1.6% cc QoQ. EBIT margins at 15.1% versus JMFL/consensus expectations of 15.6%/15.5%. Forward looking indicators – i) LTM order inflow grew 10.2% YoY in FY26 versus 7% YoY growth in FY25 (LTM book to bill of 1.4x in FY26 versus 1.3x in FY25). ii) Headcount remained stable on a sequential basis (+4.3% YoY). iii) Management commentary – confident to continue the growth momentum in FY27. However, due to certain macroeconomic environment that might happen within the three months period, there could be some softness in a particular quarter. Valuations are at ~21x FY27 consensus EPS. LTM is at ~7% premium to Mphasis for a similar growth profile in FY27, as per consensus. We reduce our EPS estimates by 3-5% over FY27-28E, incorporating 4Q results. Maintain target multiple of 19x Sep 27 – revised TP of INR 4,115 (versus INR 4,285 earlier). Maintain REDUCE.

UTI AMC | Reports loss due to other income; all is well<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21217&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              ADD                 INR 1,100

UTI AMC reported a loss of INR 514mn in 4Q only due to MTM losses resulting in negative other income of INR 1,630mn. Operating performance was sound; revenue fell just 1% QoQ while QAAUM was down 3%. Employee expenses were back to the trend rate of INR 1.3bn while other expenses were elevated with marketing efforts. In the concall, the CEO (who took over w.e.f. Feb’26) highlighted the focus on building an agile salesforce and a product suite that can deliver across market cycles to ultimately deliver “growth”. We believe we should look out in this quarter for the structural rejuvenation of India’s oldest AMC under new leadership and a vibrant team. We raise our TP slightly to INR 1,100 (from INR 1,050 earlier), valuing the AMC at 14x FY27E EPS of INR 78 (against 13x FY28E EPS of INR 82 earlier). We maintain ADD.

Indian Energy Exchange | 4QFY26 first cut: In-line; coupling overhang continues<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21216&DownloadType=show>

Flash Update

IEX reported in-line results, with trading volumes growing 24% YoY. FY26 market clearing prices in the DAM (-14% YoY) and RTM (-16% YoY) declined due to subdued power demand and increase in supply side liquidity. Key highlights from the 4QFY26 results are as follows- Revenue increased 23% YoY to INR 1.7 bn (-3% vs. JMFe / +3% consensus), driven by 24% YoY growth in trading volumes.- EBITDA margin reported at 85.7% vs. 85.3% / 86.1% in 4QFY25 / JMFe.- PAT came at INR 1.3 bn (11% YoY / -9% JMFe / -4% Cons), due to lower than expected other income (-32% YoY / -41% JMFe).- Market clearing prices in the DAM (Day-Ahead Market) and RTM (Real-Time Market) stood at INR 3.86 per unit and INR 3.59 per unit, down 14% and 16% YoY due to increased supply liquidity on the exchange.- The Board of Directors announced a final dividend of INR 2.0 per share.

Tips Music | Strong end to FY26; steady guidance for FY27<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21214&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              ADD                 INR 730

Tips Music reported strong 4QFY26 earnings growth of 92.9% YoY, driven by 32.4% revenue growth (19.1%/10.7%/21.4% in 1Q/2Q/3QFY26) and ~2,660bps EBIT margin expansion. Top-line growth was supported by sustained traction in the company’s catalogue, particularly its 90s repertoire, with management reiterating that growth was entirely recurring with no one-offs. Paid subscription contribution has inched up to 10–15% of digital revenue (10–12% last year). Margin expansion was largely led by lower content spends (down 54.6% YoY) due to deferment of a key movie-based music content release to 1QFY27. We broadly factor in management’s revenue guidance of ~20% revenue growth in FY27 (up from 15% expected earlier) but build content spends at the lower end of the guidance of INR 800mn-900mn basis historical under-delivery. Overall, we raise FY27E–28E EPS by 12–15%. We roll-forward to Mar’27 TP for a revised TP of INR 730 (INR 580 earlier) as we now value the stock at 32x P/E versus 30x earlier.

Bharat Electronics | Order momentum strong; valuations expensive<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21213&DownloadType=show>

Company Update                     ADD                 INR 485

Bharat Electronics (BEL) is well placed to benefit from rising capex in India’s defence electronics. In our view, it could be a key beneficiary of electronic components, avionics and radar orders across large defence platforms. The company has secured order inflows of INR 300bn in FY26 (+60% YoY) ahead of its guidance of INR 270bn. We expect this ordering momentum to persist in FY27 too, with strong prospects of over INR 500bn. We forecast revenue/EPS CAGR of 16% each over FY26–28E. However, BEL’s valuation discount to capital goods growth stocks is close to an all-time low (discount at 8% versus 10-year average of 30%). In our view, the current stock price largely reflects these positives. We value BEL at 44x FY28 EPS of INR 11, arriving at our revised TP of INR 485 and assume coverage with an ADD rating.

Bharat Heavy Electricals | Journey of growth and profit begins<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21221&DownloadType=show>

Company Update                     BUY                 INR 393

BHEL has run up 32% in just one month versus 20%/19% for S&P BSE Industrials/Power index. We believe order book has not yet peaked given 18/14GW of pipeline along with improving non-thermal visibility. With the commissioning of 8.9/10GW of legacy projects, pickup in both execution and margin is certain in our view. Moreover, we reckon the permission to import from China, change in product mix in non-thermal orders (HVDC, naval guns, nuclear), indigenisation of supercritical and op-lev would propel margins to mid-teens over next two–three years. Easing of working capital stress is also likely considering large customer advances and commissioning of legacy projects. The stock is trading at 27x FY28E EPS (consensus) at Q4FY26E EBITDA margin of 11% (consensus). We anticipate a beat on margins, making it worthy of an earnings upgrade and re-rating as revenues compound 20% over FY25–28E while EBITDA margin expands from 4.4% to 11.2% in base case and RoE improves to at least 15% by FY28E (versus 25–30% over 2008–12 cycle). These factors call for a premium in our view; accordingly, we value the stock at a P/E of 32x (up from 30x), yielding a revised TP of INR 393 (INR 345 earlier); reiterate ‘BUY’.

Havells India | 4Q a beat, but how sustainable is it?<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21211&DownloadType=show>

Rating Downgrade                   ADD                 INR 1,490

Havells missed 4Q revenue estimate but beat EBITDA/PAT estimates given (1) stronger C&W margin, and (2) lower-than-expected losses in Lloyd despite revenue declining 19% YoY. Within C&W, Havells reported ~6% volume growth, with wires (higher share of copper) volume declining while cables (mix of copper and aluminium) volume rose minimally; increasing price trends aided margin. The company indicated demand has improved off late in Lloyd after a weak start to the summer and inventory may normalise only by end-April. Further, within ECD, despite a YoY revenue decline of ~3%, commentary suggested no market share losses. Lastly, on margin, we note a medium-term aspiration to gain 200bps. That said, we build in a conservative 100bps expansion over FY26–29E given (1) Increased investments in A&P, brand visibility initiatives, and R&D, and (2) unlikelihood of C&W segment margin sustaining as there were gains from rising input costs in 4Q. We cut our FY27/28E estimates by ~3% and value Havells at 45x Mar’28E EPS, arriving at a TP of INR 1,490, implying ~10% upside to CMP. Hence, downgrade to ADD.

SBI Life Insurance | Best bet in an uncertain demand environment<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21208&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              BUY                 INR 2,200

SBI Life reported Q4FY26 VNB of INR 16.3bn, +2% JMFe, -2% YoY. Individual APE/total APE growth of 8%/6% YoY was below estimates, while margin surprised positively though it declined 210bps YoY to 28.3%. Management had expected a 1.74% full-year impact from GST 2.0 for this year, but it was lower at 1.50%. FY26 APE growth and margin were in line with guidance at 13%/27.5%. Management guided for 14% APE growth and 27–28% VNB margin for FY27E and the near term. We have hardly changed our VNB estimates; we slightly cut APE growth but raise margin forecasts. We maintain BUY with a revised TP of INR 2,200, valuing the stock at 1.9x Mar’28E EVPS of INR 1,158 (against 1.9x Mar’28E EVPS of INR 1,135 earlier). March industry volume data and these 4Q results reinforce our view that SBI Life is the best play in life insurance (a defensive sector in itself) when there is demand uncertainty; it remains our top pick in the space.

Tech Mahindra | Inline 4Q results; FY27 margin trajectory remains on track<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21209&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              ADD                 INR 1,555

Tech Mahindra reported 4QFY26 results – revenues and margins were largely inline. Revenue growth came in at 0.6% cc QoQ vs expectation of 0.4% cc QoQ. EBIT margins at 13.8% – inline versus JMFL and consensus expectations. Forward looking indicators – i) LTM new TCV grew ~42% YoY in FY26 versus ~43% YoY new TCV growth in FY25 (LTM book to bill of 0.59x in FY26 versus 0.43x in FY25). ii) Headcount saw a slight decline -1.3% QoQ / -0.7% YoY. iii) Management commentary – confident of doing better than the industry growth and achieve 15% EBIT margins in FY27. Industry growth is expected to be 2-4% or 3-5%. Tech Mahindra has executed well on margins – already factored in the valuations, in our view. Valuations are at 19x FY27 consensus EPS, at c.7% premium to the sector. We revise our EPS estimates marginally over FY27-28E, incorporating 4Q results. Maintain target multiple of 19x Sep 27 – revised TP of INR 1,555 (versus INR 1,550 earlier). Maintain ADD.

Tata Communications | Earnings beat on strong digital portfolio growth<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21210&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              BUY                 INR 1,980

Tata Communications (TCOM) reported consolidated revenue of INR 65.5bn (up 5.9% QoQ and 9.4% YoY) for Q4FY26, higher than JMFe/consensus, led by strong growth across digital portfolio, core connectivity, voice segments and TCR. Data segment revenue was 4.2% higher than JMFe at INR 56.8bn (up 6.1% QoQ and 11.5% YoY) as digital portfolio revenue was 6.7% higher than JMFe at INR 29.1bn (up 9.4% QoQ and 19.2% YoY) and core connectivity revenue was also 1.7% higher at INR 27.7bn (up 2.8% QoQ and 4.5% YoY). However, consolidated EBITDA, at INR 12.8bn (up 4.5% QoQ and 14.4% YoY), was only 0.9-2.6% higher than JMFe/consensus due to higher network and other opex, resulting in EBITDA margin of 19.6% in Q4FY26 (19.8% in 3QFY26). Net debt fell QoQ to INR 94bn while capex fell QoQ to INR 5.4bn or 8.3% of revenue in 4Q. We maintain our BUY rating on TCOM (unchanged TP of INR 1,980/share) based on 10x FY28 EV/EBITDA multiple for the data segment (5Y historical average of 11x) as we expect data segment EBITDA to grow at a robust CAGR of ~20% over FY26–29E, driven by a) strong growth in digital portfolio, positioned strongly across key megatrends like cloud, AI, IoT, etc.; b) improvement in overall EBITDA margin from current ~20% to ~22% by FY28 (management guidance of 23–25%) as operating leverage and acquisition synergies are likely to be partly offset by adverse revenue mix.

Sunteck Realty | Strong pre-sales with improving collections<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21207&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              BUY                 INR 560

Sunteck Realty (SRL) posted robust FY26 pre-sales of c. INR 31.6bn (+25% YoY) with Q4FY26 at c. INR 10.6bn (+22% YoY; +45% QoQ; -4% JMFe), underpinned by sustained traction in premium and uber-luxury segments. Quarterly collections increased 39% YoY to c. INR 4.3bn; for FY26, collections rose 14% YoY to INR 14.3bn with an operating surplus of INR 5.5bn (+48% YoY). Collections are set to improve meaningfully over FY27/FY28 as execution picks up pace. Business development accelerated during the year with investments in land increasing to c. INR 8bn (from c. INR 1.8bn in FY25) and the addition of c. INR 50bn GDV across three new projects, with embedded margins of c. 30–35%. The launch pipeline remains healthy (c. INR 70bn GDV for FY27), including subsequent phases at Vasai and Naigaon alongside a recently signed JDA project in Mira Road. The Dubai project is launch-ready, subject to geopolitical normalisation. Overall, management expresses confidence in sustaining current pre-sales momentum into FY27, irrespective of the Dubai project launch. Pre-sales could expand at 27% CAGR over FY26–28E on the back of multiple launches. We reiterate BUY with a DCF based Mar’27E TP of INR 560.

PNC Infratech | Receives much awaited NHAI orders; backlog strengthens<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21204&DownloadType=show>

Company Update                     BUY                 INR 345

PNC Infratech (PNC) has been declared L1 by the NHAI in two HAM road projects for 4-laning of the Barabanki–Biswariya stretch in Uttar Pradesh; this would have a total EPC value of c. INR 30bn. And given the packages are contiguous, we expect them to support margins. These wins are particularly positive as NHAI order inflows had remained slow over the past two years— for instance, PNC’s FY26 inflows aggregated INR 60bn versus guidance of INR 120bn. Now these fresh wins will strengthen order backlog and improve execution visibility. Order backlog stood at a strong INR 203bn (2.9x TTM revenue) at end-Dec-25. All in all, we reckon a robust EPS CAGR of 22% over FY25–28E. Meanwhile, the stock is trading at an attractive 10x/8x FY27E/28E EPS. All in all, our valuation and TP remain unchanged—we value the EPC business at 13x FY27E EPS yielding an SotP-based TP of INR 345; maintain BUY.

ABB India | Parent reported order inflows, which seems largely inline<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21206&DownloadType=show>

Flash Update

ABB India’s parent entity ABB Global has reported 1QCY26 numbers with overall 24% YoY growth in order inflows on a comparable basis (possibly adjusting for sale of Robotics & Automation). A positive read-through for India is the reported 1QCY26 order inflow of +26% YoY, the highest in the key AMEA region for ABB Global. This appears in-line with our estimate of INR43bn (preview note) of ~27% YoY order inflow (ex. Robotics) for ABB India in 1QCY26E. That said, margins may remain under pressure for ABB India. Moreover, current valuations make the risk reward profile significantly adverse, in our view.

Strategy | JM India Pulse – Mar’26<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21205&DownloadType=show>

Sector Update

In this note, we lay out the most important monthly market, sectoral and economic indicators tracked by the JM Financial research team. The indicators span high-frequency macro variables and sector-level operating metrics that help assess near-term growth momentum and emerging inflection points. Escalation in the West Asia conflict sent Brent crude oil price soaring past the USD 100/bbl mark. FII selling intensified and the INR weakened; however, the trade deficit narrowed in Mar’26. The domestic environment reflects selective strength led by improving discretionary consumption and credit growth amid easing financial conditions. Household financialisation remains resilient even as digital payment growth normalises. Industrial activity moderated slightly with capex remaining uneven. Rising oil and gas prices, supply disruptions and geopolitical uncertainty have driven the recent volatility in Indian equity markets, though peace talks between the US and Iran have improved market sentiment.

Best regards
